name: RABBIT-FEATURE-BUG-5
title: Step 4 bypass warning in SKILL.md uses plain [rabbit] prefix instead of [rabbit emoji] brand string
description:
Sibling to closed RABBIT-FEATURE-BUG-1 (split by feature scope).

The rabbit-feature-touch SKILL.md instructs the dispatcher to emit a Step 4 bypass warning with the plain text '[rabbit]' prefix:

  [rabbit] Step 4 SKIPPED: .rabbit-human-approval-bypass marker active. Run /rabbit-config human-approval true to restore the gate and require approval again.

The canonical brand prefix is the emoji-framed form '[(rabbit-emoji) rabbit (rabbit-emoji)]' per contract Inv 35/36 and tdd-subagent Inv 5. LLM-emitted operational messages should follow the same convention so users see one consistent prefix across script and dispatcher output.

Fix (this feature only):
- .claude/features/rabbit-feature/skills/rabbit-feature-touch/SKILL.md line 98: replace '[rabbit] Step 4 SKIPPED:' with the emoji-framed form
- .claude/features/rabbit-feature/docs/spec/spec.md Inv 9 (line 145): update wording from 'visible [rabbit] warning' to the emoji-framed form so spec sanctions the brand prefix
- Add regression test asserting SKILL.md Step 4 bypass warning string uses emoji brand and does NOT contain bare '[rabbit]' prefix

Companion bug for the tdd-subagent side of the fix tracks dispatch-tdd-subagent.py preamble + tdd-subagent spec Inv 17 + the test that hard-asserts the bare prefix. They land as two separate PRs.
